# PASTA Checklist: PASTA Threat Modeling

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, completeness, and consistency of the written requirements for the PASTA threat-modeling feature before implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-06-07
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)
**Focus**: Broad feature quality
**Audience**: PR reviewer
**Depth**: Standard

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are requirements defined for all seven PASTA stages, including what each stage must capture beyond its name alone? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the stage-specific minimum-content thresholds complete enough for each PASTA stage to support repeatable unlock decisions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003A, Spec §FR-003B]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements explicit about what context is reused from existing threat models during ordinary PASTA use versus bootstrap from STRIDE-LM? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-013A]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for the full bootstrap outcome, including which source-model artifacts are copied, linked, or left behind? [Gap, Spec §FR-013A, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements complete for both interactive review and the HTML, PDF, and CSV export surfaces required for v1? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-016B]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements defined for how selected PASTA findings become downstream threat scenarios, including generation and linking paths? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016A, Spec §FR-020A]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Are the defined minimum-content thresholds specific enough that reviewers can tell when each stage should unlock the next one? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003B]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "where possible" for STRIDE-LM reuse translated into objective eligibility rules instead of relying on team judgment at implementation time? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK009 Are the defined revalidation triggers complete enough to determine when later-stage content must move back into needs-revalidation status? [Clarity, Spec §FR-018A, Spec §FR-018B, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK010 Do the clarification, user-story, requirement, success-criteria, and assumption sections consistently reference HTML, PDF, and CSV as the v1 export surfaces? [Clarity, Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-016B, Spec §SC-003A]
- [ ] CHK011 Is the distinction between a PASTA stage record, a PASTA finding, and a standard downstream threat scenario expressed without overlap or role confusion? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §FR-007A, Spec §FR-016A]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Do the stage names remain consistent across user stories, functional requirements, localization impact, and success criteria? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Localization Impact]
- [ ] CHK013 Are the bootstrap-only methodology requirements consistent across clarifications, edge cases, and FR-013A/FR-013B? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-013A, Spec §FR-013B, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK014 Do the review-surface requirements align with the success criteria and assumptions about existing export-surface reuse? [Consistency, Spec §FR-016B, Spec §SC-003A, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK015 Are the downstream-linking requirements consistent with the requirement to preserve PASTA artifacts as methodology-specific records? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007A, Spec §FR-016A, Spec §FR-020A]
- [ ] CHK016 Are the authorization and audit requirements aligned with the described bootstrap, stage-edit, export, and downstream-link behaviors? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015, Spec §Security, Audit, and Operations]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 Can each primary user story be verified from written acceptance scenarios without inferring hidden stage, finding, or export behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Stories 1-3]
- [ ] CHK018 Are the success criteria measurable without relying on implementation-specific internals or unlisted test fixtures? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-003A]
- [ ] CHK019 Does SC-002 define the reference validation scenario precisely enough for timed reviews to be repeatable across reviewers? [Clarity, Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK020 Can reviewers objectively determine from the spec whether a PASTA model is "completed" versus merely reviewable but incomplete? [Measurability, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-018, Spec §SC-001]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements written for the full lifecycle of a new PASTA model: create, progress, revisit, review, export, and downstream operationalization? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 1-3, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-016A]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements defined for both direct PASTA creation and one-way bootstrap from an existing STRIDE-LM model? [Coverage, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-013A]
- [ ] CHK023 Are reviewer scenarios covered for partially completed models as distinctly as completed models? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK024 Are non-STRIDE-mapped PASTA findings covered as a first-class scenario rather than only as an exception? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-010]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK025 Are requirements explicit about the expected behavior when bootstrap source data is incomplete, stale, or only partially reusable? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-013A, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK026 Are requirements defined for what happens to linked/generated downstream scenarios when the source PASTA finding is later revised, invalidated, or removed? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-020A]
- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements defined for conflicting or duplicate STRIDE-LM mappings on the same finding, including whether ordering or precedence matters? [Edge Case, Clarity, Spec §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK028 Are requirements explicit about how export output should represent locked or needs-revalidation stages without misleading reviewers? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-016B]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK029 Are audit requirements defined with enough specificity to distinguish which user actions require separate events or attributable history entries? [Non-Functional, Clarity, Spec §FR-015, Spec §Audit/Event Impact]
- [ ] CHK030 Are localization requirements defined broadly enough to cover stage workflow copy, revalidation messaging, bootstrap prompts, and export terminology consistently? [Non-Functional, Completeness, Spec §FR-017, Spec §Localization Impact]
- [ ] CHK031 Are performance expectations for stage editing and export review defined clearly enough to prevent regressions against current threat-model workflows? [Gap, Non-Functional, Spec §SC-002, Spec §Success Criteria]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK032 Are assumptions about reuse of existing threat-model assets, exports, audit patterns, and downstream mitigation flows validated rather than left implicit? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK033 Is the dependency on existing threat export surfaces documented precisely enough to show which surfaces are in scope for v1 and which may be deferred? [Dependency, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-016B, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK034 Are dependencies on existing threat-model permissions and downstream workflows described clearly enough to assess whether any role or contract changes are required? [Dependency, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-020]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK035 Does the Security, Audit, and Operations section maintain one authoritative Migration/Backfill statement and one authoritative Localization statement? [Consistency, Spec §Security, Audit, and Operations]
- [ ] CHK036 Is it unambiguous whether existing per-scenario PASTA fields are part of the long-term requirement scope or only a migration concern to be handled later? [Ambiguity, Gap, Spec §Migration/Backfill Impact]
- [ ] CHK037 Do the requirements treat the v1 export-scope decision as settled product scope rather than as a planning-time feasibility question? [Consistency, Spec §FR-016B, Spec §SC-003A, Spec §Assumptions]

## Notes

- This checklist focuses on the written requirements, not on validating code behavior.
- Items emphasize completeness, clarity, consistency, and traceability for PR review of the current remediated feature spec.
